meta.title: Shape-Memory Latent Representation (SMLR) Framework
meta.short_tagline: A unified framework mapping material physics to latent dynamics for reversible, energy-based adaptation in artificial intelligence.
source_papers: A Thermal, Mechanical, and Materials Framework for a Shape Memory Alloy Heat Engine
Memory Formation in Matter (Rev. Mod. Phys.)
The Reversible Residual Network: Backpropagation Without Storing Activations

overview.title: The Dual Foundations of State
overview.intro: The Shape-Memory Latent Representation (SMLR) framework establishes a rigorous conceptual bridge between the thermodynamic transformations of physical materials and the adaptive processes of computational intelligence. By mapping the crystallographic phases of alloys like Nitinol to latent representations, SMLR introduces a physics-based approach to model stability and plasticity.

summary_card.label: Core Thesis
summary_card.title: State as Energy
summary_card.body: Computational intelligence is reframed as a series of thermodynamic state changes where learning is 'deformation' and forgetting is 'restoration.' The framework utilizes the Austenite-R-Phase-Martensite (A-R-M) cycle to manage model adaptation with explicit energy costs.

key_concepts.title: Thermodynamic Principles in Latent Space

cards.title: Austenite Ground State (z_0)
cards.body: The high-symmetry, minimum-energy 'parent' state of a model. It represents pre-trained core knowledge that is stable, rigid, and thermodynamically protected from spurious perturbations.

cards.title: Physical Hysteresis as Memory
cards.body: Memory is not stored as an external vector but as a path-dependent state in a hysteretic energy landscape. The model's current behavior is a direct function of its contextual history and the energy barriers it has overcome.

cards.title: Clausius-Clapeyron Plasticity Control
cards.body: A governing equation for the stability-plasticity dilemma. By tuning a computational 'temperature' parameter (τ), the framework allows for real-time control over the critical stress required to trigger model adaptation.

key_concepts.intro: These concepts shift machine learning from stateless operations to stateful physical transformations.

workflow.title: The SMLR Adaptive Cycle

steps.label: Canonical Encoding
steps.summary: Mapping input data into the stable Austenite basin.
bullets: Input x is compressed via encoder φ to z_0.
Initial state resides at the global minimum of the internal energy function.

steps.label: Contextual Stress Application
steps.summary: Injecting contextual perturbations to drive phase shifts.
bullets: Deform the energy landscape to create new local minima (Martensite zones).

steps.label: Thermodynamic Restoration
steps.summary: Reverting adapted states back to the canonical ground state.
bullets: Force z_c to overcome hysteresis barriers and relax back to z_0.

workflow.intro: An operational process for managing model states through energy landscapes.

checklist.title: SMLR Implementation Protocol

items.label: Energy Function Design
bullets: Define the internal energy function to stabilize z_0.

items.label: Temperature Calibration
bullets: Set τ to balance robustness and sensitivity.
Implement dynamic τ scaling based on task novelty.

items.label: Hysteresis Mapping
bullets: Measure the area of the hysteresis loop for memory depth.

checklist.intro: Essential steps for engineers to deploy shape-memory architectures.

metrics.intro: Quantifying the efficiency of thermodynamic state changes.

items.name: Restoration Fidelity
items.description: The accuracy with which a model returns to z_0 after deep adaptation.

items.name: Critical Stress Threshold
items.description: The minimum context magnitude required to trigger a phase transition.

metrics.title: Performance Indicators

failure_modes.name: Metastable Trap
failure_modes.description: Model becomes 'stuck' in an adapted Martensite state due to insufficient restoration energy.

failure_modes.name: Semantic Fatigue
failure_modes.description: Repeated transformations degrade the sharpness of the Austenite attractor.
mitigations: Periodic re-regularization of the ground state

resilience.intro: Addressing the risks inherent in physicalized latent dynamics.
resilience.title: Failure Modes & Mitigations

structure.intro: Comparative classification of the three-state physical model.
